signal-orange.$description: Signal Orange — Full-bleed section surfaces, geometric illustration caps, partner section canvas — carries warmth and authority across an otherwise achromatic text system

seafoam-panel.$description: Seafoam Panel — Hero canvas and alternating section ground — a cool counterweight to the warm orange, used as full-bleed background, never as a text highlight

cream-stock.$description: Cream Stock — Soft band surfaces between content sections — a paper-like off-white warmer than pure #ffffff, signals a transition zone

charcoal.$description: Charcoal — Filled button background, dark illustration blocks, heading accents — the loaded weight of the palette, softer than pure black

ink.$description: Ink — Primary body and heading text, hairline borders, icon strokes, link underlines — the dominant typographic color

pebble.$description: Pebble — Muted border and separator color for subtle structural lines that shouldn't compete with text

paper.$description: Paper — Default page canvas, text on dark filled buttons, surface for content sections between color bands

maax-mono.$description: Primary text and body — monospaced at body size gives the whole site a code-readout, technical-manual cadence. This is the signature choice: a service company writing like a terminal

sofia-regular.$description: Display headings — a softer humanist sans used sparingly for larger section titles, providing the only non-monospaced typographic moment in the system

helvetica.$description: Micro UI text — marquee strips, tiny labels, and small interface markers; falls back to system monospaced where available

paper.$description: Surface level 1: Default content canvas for text-heavy sections

cream-stock.$description: Surface level 2: Warm transition band between content blocks

seafoam-panel.$description: Surface level 3: Hero and alternate section canvas — the cool beat

signal-orange.$description: Surface level 4: Full-bleed partner/spectacle section — the loud beat
